articles.title: ‘Not Against Iran...’: US Ally UAE ‘Pleads’ For Putin’s Help After IRGC’s FINAL ‘WARNING SHOTS’
articles.description: While the U.S. and Israel navigate a volatile military standoff with Iran, Vladimir Putin is playing the ultimate diplomatic card. In a critical phone call, UAE President Mohammed bin Zayed personally assured Putin that Emirati territory is not being used to launch attacks against Iran. With UAE infrastructure taking a brutal beating from Iranian retaliatory strikes, Putin has offered to act as a direct backchannel messenger to Tehran. As Washington beats the drums of war, Moscow is proving it’s the only major power that can still speak to all sides. Watch the analysis.

articles.title: Bill Belichick’s girlfriend Jordon Hudson questions why major sports media outlets continue ignoring the Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini scandal
articles.description: Jordon Hudson has renewed criticism of major NFL media outlets over their handling of the Mike Vrabel-Dianna Russini controversy. Hudson argues that networks aggressively covered her relationship with Bill Belichick while avoiding detailed discussion about allegations involving Vrabel and Russini. Her comments gained traction after she reposted a montage highlighting media reluctance to address the story. The debate has now expanded into broader questions about consistency, newsroom priorities, and double standards in NFL coverage.

articles.title: Bhojshala row: Digvijaya Singh slams BJP for 'Hindu-Muslim divide'
articles.description: Digvijaya Singh slammed the BJP over the Bhojshala row, accusing it of a "Hindu-Muslim divide" agenda. He cited ASI rules and the Places of Worship Act, 1991, arguing against changing the monument's religious status, which is a punishable offence.

articles.title: Chinese Proverb of the Day: “The woman who tells her age is either too young to have anything to lose or too old to have anything to gain” — Life lessons on perception, social expectations, identity, experience and why confidence speaks louder than age
articles.description: Chinese Proverb of the Day highlights the meaning of “The woman who tells her age is either too young to have anything to lose or too old to have anything to gain.” The saying reflects how age is often shaped by social perception, expectations, and personal confidence rather than just a number. It suggests that people may choose to reveal or conceal their age depending on how they feel society will judge them.

articles.title: Indian Army chief’s warning to Pakistan: Decide whether you want to be part of geography or history
articles.description: Army Chief Upendra Dwivedi issued a stern warning to Pakistan regarding its support for cross-border terrorism, stating Islamabad must choose between being part of geography or history. He also lauded India's young generation for their technological prowess and adaptability, highlighting the Army's internship initiative and the critical need for military modernization and a whole-of-nation approach to future warfare.

articles.title: 'Cooperate With Us Or...': Iran To Collect Fees For Crossing Strait Of Hormuz? Shock Reveal
articles.description: Iran is set to tighten its grip over the Strait of Hormuz, the world's most critical oil chokepoint. Ebrahim Azizi, head of the Iranian parliament’s national security committee, announced that Tehran has finalized a designated route system for vessels passing through the waterway. According to Iranian officials, only commercial ships and countries cooperating with Iran will benefit from the arrangement, while fees will be charged for “specialized services” provided under the mechanism. The development follows earlier remarks by Iran’s new supreme leader Mojtaba Khamenei, who unveiled a new “Hormuz rulebook” with suggestions including receiving payments from ships crossing the Strait in Iranian rials.

articles.title: Demand for circuit bench: Chief Justice of Karnataka HC likely to visit Mangaluru this month-end or next month, says MLC
articles.description: Chief Justice of Karnataka High Court to visit Mangaluru soon to assess setting up a circuit bench, says MLC.

articles.title: “There’s So Much More To You Than The Way Your Body Looks” — Gauthami Jeji
articles.description: Growing up with vitiligo taught Gauthami Jeji how quickly the world turns bodies into public conversation. Now, she’s rejecting performative body positivity and embracing body neutrality as a softer, more honest way of living inside the body.

articles.title: Trump says Iran ceasefire was a 'favour to Pakistan', other nations
articles.description: Donald Trump stated the US-Iran ceasefire was a 'favour to Pakistan' and other nations. He also suggested China's Xi Jinping could pressure Tehran over the Strait of Hormuz, while US officials reportedly distrust Pakistan's role as a mediator.

articles.title: NEET-UG 2026 leak: NSUI protests NTA, demands minister's resignation
articles.description: NSUI members protested outside the NTA office following the NEET-UG 2026 paper leak. They demanded a ban on the agency and the resignation of Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan. Several protestors were detained by police after a scuffle broke out.

articles.title: India Vs Pakistan: How the two nations stack up when it comes to nuclear arsenals
articles.description: India and Pakistan possess nearly identical nuclear arsenals, yet their doctrines diverge significantly. India adheres to 'No First Use' and aims for a minimum deterrent, while Pakistan's 'Full Spectrum Deterrence' counters India's conventional strength. India is expanding its nuclear triad, focusing on survivability and reach, while Pakistan emphasises tactical nuclear weapons and flexible options, raising escalation risks.

articles.title: DMK's Post-Poll Defeat: Stalin forms 38-member committee for review
articles.description: After DMK's defeat in the 2026 TN polls, ex-CM MK Stalin formed a 38-member committee for a ground-level study. He instructed them to report workers' true views, even criticism against him, to enable party reforms by the end of June.

articles.title: Trump says US won't travel 9500 miles to fight Taiwan's war for independence
When a boss says “Let’s circle back,” what employees may already expect
articles.description: Corporate jargon like 'let's circle back' often masks indecision. Employees interpret this phrase as a delay tactic, not a genuine need for more information. This ambiguity breeds frustration and erodes trust in management. Clear communication, timelines, and ownership are vital for effective collaboration and employee engagement in today's workplace.
